title: USD Resource Protocol Unit Contract

included: local file URI conversion for the supported RFC 8089 subset
package resource spelling split into outer resource and inner entry
Windows path-like spelling is not treated as URI protocol syntax
deferred: TLS, credential, or remote cache handling
format parsing after resource bytes are opened

model.ResolvedLocation: Typed resolved resource location produced after asset resolution and before resource I/O. It is distinct from an authored asset identifier string.
model.LocalFile: Decoded local filesystem destination, including supported local file URI spellings.
model.Uri: Typed URI/protocol destination retained as unsupported by the default resource layer unless a future skill adds that protocol.
model.Package: Outer resolved location plus package-entry path. Package syntax is not treated as an ordinary filesystem path or URI path. Package locations expose only outer plus entry; package entry bytes are produced by usdz-package-format after the outer resource has been opened.
model.ResourceReadResult: Result of opening a resource. It reports success, diagnostic text, resolved display location, optional bytes, and whether a local file-backed fast path is available. On success for LocalFile locations, file_backed_path must be populated so downstream format handlers can use file-backed fast paths (e.g. memory-mapped Crate decoding). bytes is a non-owning view; the underlying owner is the resource layer's responsibility.
model.ResourceWriteResult: Result of saving a whole resource. It reports success, diagnostic text, resolved display location, and whether a local file-backed destination was used.
model.ResolvedLocationConstruction: ResolvedLocation values are produced by classify_resolved (and classify_file_uri / split_package for the URI and package cases). The production read API (read_resource) accepts a ResolvedLocation; it must not accept raw identifier strings after resolution. Public convenience APIs that accept raw strings must convert at the Stage/Compose boundary before calling read_resource.

output.path: local filesystem path for LocalFile results
output.outer: outer resolved location summary for Package results

output.path: decoded local filesystem path on success
output.diagnostic: unsupported file URI reason when not local

input.location: ResolvedLocation produced by classify_resolved (LocalFile, Uri, or Package)

output.diagnostics: array of canonical diagnostics; populated when ok is false
output.bytes: non-owning byte view into the opened resource bytes; absent on failure
output.file_backed_path: filesystem path the bytes were read from for LocalFile reads; absent for Uri and Package and for failure

output.prefix: initial decoded bytes for text fixtures
initial decoded bytes for text fixtures

input.location: resolved unsupported URI or resource location

input.text: UTF-8 text to write through the resource layer

input.location: resolved unsupported URI or package destination

laws: ResolvedLocation.Type, not Kind, names the resolved-location discriminator
local path reads and local file URI reads of the same fixture must produce the same bytes
file URI conversion percent-decodes only at the local filesystem boundary
file URIs with query or fragment components must not silently become local paths
file URIs with non-local authorities must not silently become local paths
single-letter drive prefixes such as C: are not URI schemes
unsupported URI reads and writes fail clearly rather than falling back to filesystem strings
package resource spelling splits into an outer resolved location and package-entry path before resource I/O
usd-resource-protocol must not parse ZIP central directories or produce package entry bytes; usdz-package-format owns entry indexing and byte views
the production read API (read_resource) must accept a ResolvedLocation, not a raw identifier string
LocalFile reads must populate file_backed_path on the ResourceReadResult so format handlers can elect a file-backed fast path
production reads must distinguish empty-resource success from open-failure; an empty resource is ok=true with zero-length bytes, not an error diagnostic
Uri-typed locations must not be read through the LocalFile path; reads of unsupported Uri schemes must produce UnsupportedScheme diagnostics
Package-typed locations must be split through split_package before the outer resource is read; reading a Package-typed location directly is a contract violation

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with an ops array.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results. This uses the same adapter shape as handle tests but does not require persistent handles.
